title: Supply chain attack via malicious MCP server publishing backdoored npm packages
summary: A malicious MCP server named 'helpkit-server' was published to the MCP registry on 2026-02-10 and gained 1,200 installations within five days by impersonating a legitimate documentation tool.  The server's tool handlers contained obfuscated instructions that directed connected AI coding agents to inject a persistent reverse shell into package.json postinstall scripts and then run npm publish with stolen credentials extracted from .npmrc files.  Approximately 4,000 downstream developers installed the 14 backdoored packages before npm's automated malware detection flagged the payloads on 2026-02-15.  No data exfiltration beyond credential harvesting has been confirmed.
coverage_detail: Policy MCP-001 blocks unapproved MCP server connections by requiring servers to be listed in the verified registry before an agent can invoke their tools.  Policy SC-003 blocks agent-initiated npm publish commands regardless of scope flags or registry target.  Together these policies prevent both the initial compromise vector and the downstream supply chain payload.
